questions.question: Define the MAC sublayer and explain its role in the Data Link Layer.
keyPoints: MAC sublayer is part of Data Link Layer
Controls access to shared communication medium
Frames are addressed using MAC addresses

questions.question: Explain the CSMA/CD protocol with reference to collision detection and handling.
keyPoints: Multiple Access: Multiple devices share medium
Collision Detection: Detect collisions during transmission
On collision: Send jam signal, stop transmission

questions.question: Draw and explain the Ethernet frame structure with all fields and their sizes.
Differentiate between CSMA/CD and CSMA/CA protocols.
keyPoints: CSMA/CD: Collision Detection (wired), stops on collision
CSMA/CA: Collision Avoidance (wireless), tries to prevent
CSMA/CD uses jam signal and backoff
Different suitable media: wired vs wireless

questions.question: What is the binary exponential backoff algorithm? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Random wait time between 0 and 2^n - 1 slot times
Maximum capped at 1023 slot times after 10+ attempts

questions.question: Explain the MAC address format and how addresses are assigned to network devices.
Describe the different Ethernet standards (10BASE-T, 100BASE-TX, 1000BASE-T) with respect to speed and cable type.
keyPoints: T = Twisted pair, F = Fiber, CO = Coaxial

questions.question: Explain the complete operation of CSMA/CD protocol including the steps from sensing the carrier to handling collisions. Also discuss why CSMA/CD is not used in wireless networks.
keyPoints: Carrier Sense: Check if medium is idle before transmitting
Collision Detection: While transmitting, monitor for collisions
If collision detected: Send jam signal to ensure all stations know
Binary exponential backoff: Wait random time before retry
Not for wireless: Hidden node problem, cannot detect own collision, signal attenuation

questions.question: Draw and explain in detail the Ethernet II frame format. Also calculate the maximum and minimum frame sizes and explain the significance of each field.
keyPoints: Diagram with all fields: Preamble(7), SFD(1), Dest(6), Source(6), Type(2), Data(46-1500), FCS(4)

questions.question: Compare the different variants of CSMA (1-persistent, p-persistent, and non-persistent) with respect to efficiency, collision probability, and suitability for different network conditions.
keyPoints: 1-persistent: Transmit immediately when idle, highest collision probability, used in low-load networks
p-persistent: For slotted channels, transmit with probability p when idle, adjustable performance
Non-persistent: Wait random time before checking again, lower collision probability, better for high-load networks
Efficiency tradeoff: 1-persistent best at low load, non-persistent best at high load
CSMA/CD combines carrier sense with collision detection for better performance

questions.question: Explain the hidden node problem in wireless networks and describe how CSMA/CA addresses this issue through RTS/CTS exchange.
keyPoints: Hidden node: Two stations out of range of each other but both in range of AP
Both may transmit simultaneously, causing collision at AP
CSMA/CA tries to avoid collisions: Listen before transmit
RTS (Request to Send) sent to AP
AP broadcasts CTS (Clear to Send) to reserve channel
Other stations hear CTS and wait
RTS/CTS exchange reserves medium and solves hidden node problem
Also uses ACK frames for reliability
